TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Saisiyat, story: SaiNr-life_waon a boong

S.FORM: rangi' niSo isahini kano' ila patawawen.
S.TRANSL: What has been making you busy now?
I'm going to plant ginger in the mountains.

S.FORM: kaSo baboeyoe'an So'o sibaeaeh ka mae'iyaeh.

S.TRANSL: Two. Are they male or female?
Manual labor for sectioning the field.

S.FORM: ah niSo baboeyoe' koza kin akoy kapamowa'.
S.TRANSL: Ah, how much ginger do you plant?
那麼大的地只種一千斤？
One thousand (only) for such a big piece of land?

S.FORM: niSo' 'aehae' siyan tintin niSo' so: mayhael pamowa' 'am komowih ila ka baba:iw 'am koza ila kin 'akoy tintin.
S.TRANSL: 你若種一千斤，那麼明年收穫要賣多少？應該很多吧。
If you're planting a thousand kilos, then after the harvest next year, how much are you going to sell? Must be lots of kilos.

S.FORM: so: kayzaeh ka kaelaehaeng 'ana'oka' ma 'am hayza' 'aehae' babaki' ila.
S.TRANSL: 如果管理得好，大概至少有一萬斤。
If managed well, then probably at least ten thousand kilos.

S.FORM: rayhil kik raami so nak haysa'an 'aehae' tal'oemaeh 'oka' nanaw kaba:iwan.
S.TRANSL: 我不知道。像今年價格都不好。
I don't know. Like this year, prices are not good.
其他的？會結果的水果類，還沒有種。

S.FORM: a kasi'aelen nonak hayza titi'an pamowa'.
S.TRANSL: 是的。我有種一些蔬菜自己吃。
Yes, I do plant a bit of vegetables for our own consumption.

S.FORM: ma ao mamowa' ka nonak kayzaeh ka kasi'aelen ra 'ini nonak.
S.TRANSL: It's better for your own consumption.

S.FORM: 'ihi'' mamowa' ka 'oka' noka pamowa'i ka baba:iw.
S.TRANSL: 你沒有種一些你可以賣的？
You don't plant anything to sell?
要賣？誰知道？要看Ataw的意思。
For selling? Who knows? (It's up to) Ataw.
Ataw is going to plant. Your child is going to plant.

S.FORM: 'ihi' sia ray taew'an kano' patawawen ni 'ataw.
S.TRANSL: What does Ataw do at home?

S.FORM: eh siska 'i'iyokan ka tatatimaehan span ka tata:a'.
S.TRANSL: 他管理一個橘子園，種菜，養雞。
He tends an orange orchard, plants vegetables, raises chickens.

S.FORM: m haysani nakhara monaseb o: hayza.
S.TRANSL: Right now, there are like ... fifty.
Wow! So many! Do you sell (them)?

S.FORM: ma so: hayza ka mae'iyaeh mwa:i' mari' ma' siba:iw.
S.TRANSL: 是啊，若有人來買他就賣。
Ah, is it easy to raise them?

S.FORM: ma'ngel so: non 'inonaka ka ka'ina'an pas'oesizo soizin mwa:i' kasisiyap ma'angel atomalan.
S.TRANSL: 母雞孵蛋的話，小雞比較慢孵出來。
It takes long for the eggs to hatch if the hen sits on them.

S.FORM: e isaza koSa'en inkakhayza'an 'am mita' ka kaspanen tataa' kamasaseez kamama'ngel.
S.TRANSL: 我們以前養的雞長得很慢。
The chicks we raised before grew very slowly.

S.FORM: 'ihi'' ai ray babih ba:iw ay hahoeran ba:iw ka kaspanan.
S.TRANSL: 從別得地方買來嗎，還是從都市買的？
Were they bought from other places, or from the city?

S.FORM: m 'ampoa' 'okay sepan ka inay hahoeran noka noka sinraehoe'a ka paseez ka 'oesizo' hara mwa:i' ila kasisiyap kamararahoe' 'ampowa' 'okay sepan ka isaa.
S.TRANSL: 你為什麼不養那種可以用機器孵蛋的？那種比較快生小雞，小雞也長得快。
Why don't you raise those which were incubated? They hatch faster and the chicks are bigger.

S.FORM: inakisaza'an sepanen 'a a hahoeran ma'iaeh koSa: somay'akoyan o:.
S.TRANSL: 都市的人都這麼說，他們養了很多。
The people from the city say so; they raise plenty of them.
It's not that they taste bad.

S.FORM: m sel lasia ray tono' koSa: nimon hiza tabo ka ba:iw tatabo.
S.TRANSL: 他們在想要餵什麼飼料。
They're thinking what you feed them.

S.FORM: ah noka isaa ta ta'asen ma.
m hiza ka tata:a' hayza' taboon noka Sakap pa'aewpil hini' ka biae'.
kayzaeh ma'an ka anoka poer'oe' inoka aewpil ma' hayza' tabo'on.
S.TRANSL: 我用蔬菜葉子，也用蕃薯葉子。
I use vegetable leaves; I also use yam leaves.

S.FORM: tabo'on noka aewpil alikih alikah ila rahoe' ki Sakap .
S.TRANSL: 餵蕃薯葉他們長的比較快。

S.FORM: hinghae' ai hiza tata:a' koSa'en kamasaseez nanaw alikah hayza'an mita' span ka tata:a' isaa kamasaseez nanaw 'anatabo:on ma' hinghae' ma'ngel sobae:oeh.
S.TRANSL: 我們之前養的雞都很小，很久才長大，不管你餵他們什麼。
The chicken we raised before were small and it took long for them to grow, whatever you feed them.

S.FORM: rangi' So'o isahini siska kano' ila.
yako 'ima mamowa' ka ka'niw hato mamaongan.
ma'an pamowa'an ititi'an nanaw okik lal'oz noka mae'iyaeh mari'in ba:iwin.
S.TRANSL: Just a bit. Not enough to sell.
(It's too troublesome) if (you plant) too much.

S.FORM: so: 'akoy 'iyaatomalan ila mae'iyaeh 'okay wa:i' mari' ma' 'aewhaey boeloe atomal ka hahila.
S.TRANSL: 種太多的話，沒人買也不好，只是浪費時間而已。
If (you plant) too many, it's not good if nobody comes to buy (them). It's just a waste of time.

S.FORM: m 'oka' ila o: boeloe' atomal ka hahila.
m 'oka' ila o: inak hini'an ma' nakhara ma'iaeh 'am si'ael ka 'imamanta' saboeh.
S.TRANSL: Like ... some people like them raw.

S.FORM: ma lasia ki:im ka 'imamanta' ka kasi'aelen isaa.

S.TRANSL: The raw ones to be dried are sold out.
採比較大的香菇比較好，這樣小的才會長出來。
It's good (to pick the bigger mushrooms so that) the little ones can grow soon.

S.FORM: m 'okik wa'isan kaba:iwan ma' isaa 'am nak'ino'.
S.TRANSL: 市場上的價格不好，我們怎麼辦？
The market price is not good. What can we do?

S.FORM: 'am 'okik potngor ka pinawka'an ka isaa rayhil o: 'am ba:iw isaa ka topon na ka'niw.
S.TRANSL: 還不夠錢去買可以吃的香菇。
It's not even enough money to cover the cost to buy the edible fungus or mushroom.

S.FORM: rangi' hiziyo' rima' naehaen ray bangol talboeyoe'.
m sia mamowa' ila ka ka'niw.

S.TRANSL: He doesn't know how to set traps.

S.FORM: m sia hasa' talboeyoe' sia hasa' tay'itol ka kahoey na.
S.TRANSL: 他不會設陷阱，也不會爬樹。
He doesn't know how to set traps, and he doesn't climb trees.

S.FORM: hiza haehoer ray kalhib patiyay pamowa'an nia ka pazay.
S.TRANSL: 他不是在蓬萊種稻米嗎？
Doesn't he plant rice there at Penglai?

S.FORM: 'oka' ila ka hahila nom matawaw ka patiyay niya'om ka pataasen naehaen.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有時間在田裡工作，需要延後。
We don't have time to work in the fields. We need to put it off first.

S.FORM: haw niya'om layanay ma' isaa matawaw ray babi.
S.TRANSL: 我們媳婦在別的地方工作。
Our daughter-in-law also works in another place.

S.FORM: ma 'oka' nom talboewah ka hiza 'oka' nom talboewah ka patiai nomsia.
S.TRANSL: There's nobody to do the weeding and the cleaning of the place.
(Doing the cleaning) during the holiday is also good.

S.FORM: a 'oem'oemaeh so to towa'isin so:elen ila nanaw.
S.TRANSL: 田地若不處理好，就會長雜草。
If one does not maintain the field, (the weeds will start to grow).

S.FORM: 'oka' ma'an hiza ka 'oem'oemaehan ra:i' ray kaminrawko'an.
S.TRANSL: No. My field is located at the foot of the hill (where there are plenty of raoko plants.)
不管種什麼東西，天氣總是熱的。
Whatever is planted, (the weather is always) hot.

S.FORM: amisan ila isaa pamowa'en ka kano'.
hara poer'oe kamapotoy isaa inak isa'an.

S.TRANSL: Then we do the planting, like that.
Only good for ourselves to eat.

S.FORM: koSa: mamowa' ka kababa:iw ma' 'oka' ila yami pamowa'.
S.TRANSL: 我們種那些不是拿來賣的。
We don't plant those for sale.

S.FORM: may hiza mamaongan anhi' sem'el ila.
S.TRANSL: 你經過那裡有沒有看到竹筍長出來？
Passing by there, have you seen the bamboo grow there?

S.FORM: hayza hayza yami 'oka' ka miyowmi kita' noka may hiza ka yami' may isaza 'okay kita' sem'el ila.
S.TRANSL: 我們在那裡種的竹子，有人經過不會注意到有沒有長出來。
The bamboo we planted there, people passing by did not notice if they have grown.

S.FORM: aewpil ka mari'in ka sakap tatabo: ka bibi'.
S.TRANSL: 蕃薯的藤都可以拿來餵鴨子。
The yam leaves are to feed the duck.

S.FORM: kahihiwa'en ila kasi'aelen kapowa'en so: tatini'in.
S.TRANSL: 鴨子大了就可以殺，為何要養到老？
The ducks can be killed as soon as they grow old. Why do you raise them until old age?
我中午的時候到達Ayon家裡。
Arrived at the (Ayon's) house at noon.
You ate lunch at Ayon's home first.
是啊。我先在他家吃午餐。
Yes. I had lunch at her house first.

S.FORM: rima' naehaen kita' lasia 'am somobaboeyoe' haw.
S.TRANSL: 吃過午餐後，我去看工人準備土壤。
(After lunch), I went to see the workers preparing to plant ginger there.
(They planted) in a downward direction then?

S.FORM: ai aki' 'ama' no aki' ki aki' ki 'a'ari' 'a'ari' ari' insiya'a minkoringan.
ari' m hai 'ihi' ay ataw ay 'ataw 'ataw ma' hisia makakreng 'atomalan.

S.TRANSL: He can get himself a wife.
Who wants to get married to him?

S.FORM: nisia nonak kita' o: tatini' o: hayza' ka howaw haysani.
S.TRANSL: 讓他自己看看。對我麼老人而言，不關我們的事。
Let him see for himself. (For us) old people, is it our business nowadays?

S.FORM: niSo korkoring hayza' ka 'ima minowa' isaa kita' ila.
S.TRANSL: Your child (Ataw) has somebody she likes.

S.FORM: so: mita' 'ana 'am hayza' nisia ka kapaylal'ozan ka katoka'anayan o: so: 'okay toeroe'ani mita' ma'an noka tatini' ma'an.
S.TRANSL: 他們怎會有緣份，若我們老人家不指給他們？
How would they have the predestination if we old people don't point it out to them?

S.FORM: 'am tikot lasia sasekla' 'am 'okik sasekla 'ita' tatini' 'anra:an nasiya.
S.TRANSL: 他們害怕。那要看我麼老人家知不知道哪些人適合他們。
They are afraid. It's up to us old people (for them) to know or not to know suitable people."

S.FORM: ma'an hayza' ila kastinoroe' ila o:.

S.TRANSL: 我們幫他們指出兩個來了。
We've pointed out two to him.
That girl wants to get married to a SaiSiyat.
She wants to get married. She ...

S.FORM: ray kapna'misan 'am holoy nakhara koSa: kayni ila lobih ray ka 'oeS'oeSoe'an ano'.
S.TRANSL: 女方在北部上班，好像不太喜歡回鄉下住。
(The girl's) working in the north. She doesn't seem to like to come back to the province.

S.FORM: ai 'am ki:im ila nisia ka ray raehoeran ka taew'an.
S.TRANSL: She is looking for a house in the city.
